1→# UI/UX Screens Specification (v0.1)
     2→
     3→**Platform:** React Native (Expo)  
     4→**Styling:** React Native built-in StyleSheet  
     5→**Target:** Personal use, mobile-first (phone testing)  
     6→**Philosophy:** Functional first, beautiful later. Clear, readable, touch-friendly.
     7→
     8→---
     9→
    10→## Screen Hierarchy & Navigation Flow
    11→
    12→```
    13→Splash/Loading
    14→  ↓
    15→┌──────────────────────────────┐
    16→│    NOT AUTHENTICATED         │
    17→├──────────────────────────────┤
    18→│  LoginScreen                 │
    19→│  RegisterScreen              │
    20→│  ↓ (on success)              │
    21→└──────────────────────────────┘
    22→  ↓
    23→┌──────────────────────────────┐
    24→│    AUTHENTICATED             │
    25→├──────────────────────────────┤
    26→│  HomeScreen                  │
    27→│  ├─→ AssessmentScreen        │
    28→│  │   ├─→ ResultsScreen       │
    29→│  │   └─→ (back to Home)      │
    30→│  └─→ Logout (back to Login)  │
    31→└──────────────────────────────┘
    32→```
    33→
    34→---
    35→
    36→## 1. SplashScreen (Optional, ~2 seconds)
    37→
    38→**Purpose:** Show loading state while app initializes
    39→
    40→**Elements:**
    41→- App logo or title: "Attachment Profile"
    42→- Loading spinner
    43→- Optional tagline: "Understanding your relationship patterns"
    44→
    45→**Behavior:**
    46→- Check if user is authenticated (token in AsyncStorage)
    47→- If yes: navigate to HomeScreen
    48→- If no: navigate to LoginScreen
    49→- Auto-hide after 2 seconds or when auth check completes
    50→
    51→---
    52→
    53→## 2. LoginScreen
    54→
    55→**Purpose:** Authenticate existing user
    56→
    57→**Elements:**
    58→1. **Header section:**
    59→   - Title: "Welcome back"
    60→   - Subtitle: "Sign in to your account"
    61→
    62→2. **Email input field:**
    63→   - Placeholder: "you@example.com"
    64→   - Keyboard: email-address
    65→   - Validation: basic email format check
    66→
    67→3. **Password input field:**
    68→   - Placeholder: "Password"
    69→   - secureTextEntry: true
    70→   - Show/hide password icon (optional)
    71→
    72→4. **"Login" button:**
    73→   - Primary color (indigo)
    74→   - Full width, padded
    75→   - Shows spinner while loading
    76→
    77→5. **Error message display:**
    78→   - Red text, visible only if login fails
    79→   - Possible messages:
    80→     - "Invalid email or password"
    81→     - "Please enter valid email"
    82→     - "Password required"
    83→     - "Network error - please try again"
    84→
    85→6. **Link to RegisterScreen:**
    86→   - "Don't have an account? Register"
    87→   - Secondary text color, underlined
    88→
    89→**Layout Example:**
    90→```
    91→┌─────────────────────────────────────┐
    92→│                                     │
    93→│      Welcome back                   │
    94→│      Sign in to your account        │
    95→│                                     │
    96→│   ┌─────────────────────────────┐   │
    97→│   │ you@example.com             │   │
    98→│   └─────────────────────────────┘   │
    99→│                                     │
   100→│   ┌─────────────────────────────┐   │
   101→│   │ ••••••••••••••              │   │
   102→│   └─────────────────────────────┘   │
   103→│                                     │
   104→│   ┌─────────────────────────────┐   │
   105→│   │       Login      [🔄]       │   │
   106→│   └─────────────────────────────┘   │
   107→│                                     │
   108→│   Don't have an account? Register   │
   109→│                                     │
   110→└─────────────────────────────────────┘
   111→```
   112→
   113→**Behavior:**
   114→- Validate email format before submit
   115→- Show error if email/password incorrect
   116→- Navigate to HomeScreen on success
   117→- Store JWT token in AsyncStorage
   118→- Optional: Remember email in local storage
   119→
   120→**Styling:**
   121→- Background: white
   122→- Input borders: light gray (1px)
   123→- Button: primary color (indigo)
   124→- Text: dark gray
   125→- Error text: red (#EF4444)
   126→- Links: primary color, underlined
   127→
   128→---
   129→
   130→## 3. RegisterScreen
   131→
   132→**Purpose:** Create new user account
   133→
   134→**Elements:**
   135→1. **Header section:**
   136→   - Title: "Create account"
   137→   - Subtitle: "Get started with your attachment journey"
   138→
   139→2. **Email input field**
   140→
   141→3. **Password input field**
   142→
   143→4. **Confirm password input field:**
   144→   - Placeholder: "Confirm password"
   145→   - Must match password field
   146→
   147→5. **"Create account" button**
   148→
   149→6. **Validation feedback:**
   150→   - Below each field (optional, live)
   151→   - Show if password is weak
   152→   - Show if passwords don't match
   153→
   154→7. **Link to LoginScreen:**
   155→   - "Already have an account? Login"
   156→
   157→**Layout Example:**
   158→```
   159→┌─────────────────────────────────────┐
   160→│                                     │
   161→│      Create account                 │
   162→│      Get started on your journey    │
   163→│                                     │
   164→│   ┌─────────────────────────────┐   │
   165→│   │ you@example.com             │   │
   166→│   └─────────────────────────────┘   │
   167→│                                     │
   168→│   ┌─────────────────────────────┐   │
   169→│   │ ••••••••••••••              │   │
   170→│   └─────────────────────────────┘   │
   171→│   ⚠ At least 8 characters           │
   172→│                                     │
   173→│   ┌─────────────────────────────┐   │
   174→│   │ ••••••••••••••              │   │
   175→│   └─────────────────────────────┘   │
   176→│                                     │
   177→│   ┌─────────────────────────────┐   │
   178→│   │   Create account [🔄]      │   │
   179→│   └─────────────────────────────┘   │
   180→│                                     │
   181→│   Already have an account? Login    │
   182→│                                     │
   183→└─────────────────────────────────────┘
   184→```
   185→
   186→**Validation Rules:**
   187→- Email: valid format required
   188→- Password: ≥ 8 characters
   189→- Confirm: must match password exactly
   190→- Real-time feedback: green checkmark when valid
   191→
   192→**Error Messages:**
   193→- ❌ "Email already in use"
   194→- ❌ "Password must be at least 8 characters"
   195→- ❌ "Passwords do not match"
   196→- ❌ "Invalid email format"
   197→- ✅ "Account created successfully!"
   198→
   199→**Behavior:**
   200→- Disable "Create account" button until all fields valid
   201→- Auto-login after registration (optional: show "Logging you in...")
   202→- Navigate to HomeScreen on success
   203→
   204→---
   205→
   206→## 4. HomeScreen
   207→
   208→**Purpose:** Main entry point; gateway to assessment and results
   209→
   210→**Elements:**
   211→1. **Header section:**
   212→   - Greeting: "Hi, [user's email]"
   213→   - Title: "Your Attachment Profile"
   214→
   215→2. **Primary action button:**
   216→   - "Start Assessment" (or "Resume Assessment" if in progress)
   217→   - Large, primary color, full width
   218→   - Icon: play button or pencil icon
   219→
   220→3. **Secondary action button:**
   221→   - "View Past Results" (disabled if no completed assessments)
   222→   - Secondary color, full width
   223→   - Icon: chart/graph icon
   224→
   225→4. **Status section (if assessment in progress):**
   226→   - "You have an in-progress assessment"
   227→   - Shows last question number: "Question 18 of 36"
   228→   - "Continue" button or "Start over" link
   229→
   230→5. **Logout button:**
   231→   - Link text in footer
   232→   - Small, secondary color
   233→
   234→**Layout Example:**
   235→```
   236→┌─────────────────────────────────────┐
   237→│  Hi, user@example.com               │
   238→│                                     │
   239→│  Your Attachment Profile            │
   240→│                                     │
   241→│  ┌─────────────────────────────┐   │
   242→│  │ ▶ Start Assessment          │   │
   243→│  └─────────────────────────────┘   │
   244→│                                     │
   245→│  ┌─────────────────────────────┐   │
   246→│  │ 📊 View Past Results        │   │
   247→│  │    (disabled)               │   │
   248→│  └─────────────────────────────┘   │
   249→│                                     │
   250→│  Or resume: Question 18 of 36       │
   251→│  ┌─────────────────────────────┐   │
   252→│  │       Continue              │   │
   253→│  └─────────────────────────────┘   │
   254→│                                     │
   255→│           Logout                    │
   256→│                                     │
   257→└─────────────────────────────────────┘
   258→```
   259→
   260→**Behavior:**
   261→- Check for in-progress assessment on load
   262→- If exists: show "Resume" option with last question number
   263→- "Start Assessment" → navigate to AssessmentScreen
   264→- "View Past Results" → (disabled for v0.1, enabled in v0.2)
   265→- "Logout" → clear token, return to LoginScreen
   266→- Optional: show last completion date if assessment completed
   267→
   268→---
   269→
   270→## 5. AssessmentScreen
   271→
   272→**Purpose:** Answer ECR-R questions one by one
   273→
   274→**Elements:**
   275→1. **Progress section:**
   276→   - Progress bar (visual fill: 5/36 complete)
   277→   - Question counter: "Question 5 of 36"
   278→   - Percentage: "14% complete"
   279→
   280→2. **Question section:**
   281→   - Question text (large, centered, readable)
   282→   - Font size: 16-18px
   283→   - Padding: adequate margins
   284→
   285→3. **Response section (7-point Likert scale):**
   286→   - 7 radio buttons or touch-friendly buttons
   287→   - Labels: "Strongly Disagree" ← → "Strongly Agree"
   288→   - Visual indication of selected answer (highlighted)
   289→   - Current selection shown clearly
   290→
   291→4. **Navigation section:**
   292→   - "Previous" button (disabled on Q1)
   293→   - "Next" button (disabled until answer selected)
   294→   - Optional: "Skip to Results" (NO - don't implement, force completion)
   295→
   296→5. **Optional features:**
   297→   - Save indicator: "Auto-saved" (shown briefly after selection)
   298→   - Question-specific help text (not needed for v0.1)
   299→
   300→**Layout Example:**
   301→```
   302→┌─────────────────────────────────────┐
   303→│                                     │
   304→│   Question 5 of 36                  │
   305→│   ████░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░ 14%   │
   306→│                                     │
   307→│  ─────────────────────────────────  │
   308→│                                     │
   309→│  "I worry a fair amount about       │
   310→│   losing my partner."               │
   311→│                                     │
   312→│  ─────────────────────────────────  │
   313→│                                     │
   314→│  ◯ 1  Strongly Disagree             │
   315→│  ◯ 2  Disagree                      │
   316→│  ◯ 3  Somewhat Disagree             │
   317→│  ◉ 4  Neutral                       │
   318→│  ◯ 5  Somewhat Agree                │
   319→│  ◯ 6  Agree                         │
   320→│  ◯ 7  Strongly Agree                │
   321→│                                     │
   322→│  ┌──────────────┬──────────────┐   │
   323→│  │  Previous    │    Next      │   │
   324→│  └──────────────┴──────────────┘   │
   325→│                                     │
   326→│  Auto-saved                         │
   327→│                                     │
   328→└─────────────────────────────────────┘
   329→```
   330→
   331→**Behavior:**
   332→- Display one question at a time
   333→- Auto-save selection to AsyncStorage after each answer
   334→- Previous button goes back (preserves answer)
   335→- Next button goes forward (only enabled if answered)
   336→- Progress bar updates with each question
   337→- On Q36: "Next" button changes to "Submit" or "See Results"
   338→- Handle screen rotations gracefully (optional)
   339→
   340→**Styling:**
   341→- Background: white or very light gray
   342→- Radio buttons: clear, large touch targets (44px minimum)
   343→- Progress bar: primary color
   344→- Selected state: primary color highlight
   345→- Typography: readable, high contrast
   346→
   347→---
   348→
   349→## 6. ResultsScreen
   350→
   351→**Purpose:** Display attachment scores and interpretation
   352→
   353→**Elements:**
   354→1. **Header section:**
   355→   - Title: "Your Results"
   356→   - Subtitle: "Understanding your attachment style"
   357→   - Optional: Date completed
   358→
   359→2. **Scores section:**
   360→   - **Anxiety Score:**
   361→     - Large number: "4.2"
   362→     - Label: "Anxiety" with brief definition
   363→     - Visual bar (1-7 scale, color-coded)
   364→   
   365→   - **Avoidance Score:**
   366→     - Large number: "3.8"
   367→     - Label: "Avoidance" with brief definition
   368→     - Visual bar (1-7 scale, color-coded)
   369→
   370→3. **Attachment Style section:**
   371→   - Large, prominent label: "Secure" (or other style)
   372→   - Color-coded background (optional)
   373→   - Icon (optional): ❤️ for secure, etc.
   374→
   375→4. **Interpretation section:**
   376→   - 3-5 sentences of warm, non-clinical interpretation
   377→   - Tailored to attachment style
   378→   - Accessible language (no jargon)
   379→
   380→5. **Growth tip section:**
   381→   - One actionable suggestion
   382→   - Specific to attachment style
   383→   - Positive, growth-oriented
   384→
   385→6. **Action buttons:**
   386→   - "Back to Home" button
   387→   - "Retake Assessment" button (secondary)
   388→   - "Share Results" (optional, v0.2)
   389→
   390→**Layout Example:**
   391→```
   392→┌─────────────────────────────────────┐
   393→│         Your Results                │
   394→│     Understanding your style        │
   395→│     Completed: Feb 6, 2025         │
   396→│                                     │
   397→│  ─────────────────────────────────  │
   398→│                                     │
   399→│        Anxiety: 4.2                 │
   400→│  ████████░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░  │
   401→│                                     │
   402→│        Avoidance: 3.8               │
   403→│  ███████░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░  │
   404→│                                     │
   405→│  ─────────────────────────────────  │
   406→│                                     │
   407→│            ❤️ SECURE                │
   408→│                                     │
   409→│  You generally feel comfortable     │
   410→│  with emotional intimacy and can    │
   411→│  balance your needs with your       │
   412→│  partner's. You tend toward trust   │
   413→│  and healthy interdependence.       │
   414→│                                     │
   415→│  💡 Growth Tip:                     │
   416→│  Practice occasional vulnerability │
   417→│  sharing to deepen connection.      │
   418→│                                     │
   419→│  ┌─────────────────────────────┐   │
   420→│  │    Back to Home             │   │
   421→│  └─────────────────────────────┘   │
   422→│                                     │
   423→│       Retake Assessment             │
   424→│                                     │
   425→└─────────────────────────────────────┘
   426→```
   427→
   428→**Interpretation Text (Example for Secure):**
   429→```
   430→"Your attachment pattern suggests you're secure in relationships. 
   431→You're generally comfortable with both closeness and autonomy, 
   432→and you tend to communicate your needs clearly. You likely feel 
   433→secure enough to be vulnerable when appropriate, and you balance 
   434→your own needs with your partner's. This is a strong foundation 
   435→for healthy, interdependent relationships."
   436→```
   437→
   438→**Growth Tips (by style):**
   439→- **Secure:** "Practice occasional vulnerability to deepen intimacy."
   440→- **Anxious-Preoccupied:** "Notice when seeking reassurance becomes a habit; practice self-soothing first."
   441→- **Dismissive-Avoidant:** "Create small moments of emotional sharing; vulnerability strengthens bonds."
   442→- **Fearful-Avoidant:** "Work on self-compassion; your conflicted feelings are valid and can be understood."
   443→
   444→**Styling:**
   445→- Background: white or soft gradient
   446→- Scores: large, bold typography
   447→- Attachment style: prominent, color-coded (optional)
   448→- Interpretation: warm tone, readable contrast
   449→- Buttons: clear call-to-action
   450→
   451→**Behavior:**
   452→- Calculate scores automatically (backend + frontend verification)
   453→- Display results immediately after submission
   454→- Allow returning to HomeScreen
   455→- Optional: download or share results (v0.2)
   456→
   457→---
   458→
   459→## Design Tokens (Consistent Across All Screens)
   460→
   461→**Colors:**
   462→```
   463→Primary: #4F46E5 (Indigo)
   464→Success: #10B981 (Green)
   465→Warning: #F59E0B (Amber)
   466→Error: #EF4444 (Red)
   467→Background: #FFFFFF (White)
   468→Surface: #F3F4F6 (Light Gray)
   469→Text: #111827 (Dark)
   470→TextSecondary: #6B7280 (Gray)
   471→Border: #D1D5DB (Light Gray)
   472→```
   473→
   474→**Spacing:**
   475→```
   476→xs: 4px
   477→sm: 8px
   478→md: 16px
   479→lg: 24px
   480→xl: 32px
   481→```
   482→
   483→**Typography:**
   484→```
   485→HeadingXL: 32px, bold
   486→HeadingL: 24px, bold
   487→HeadingM: 20px, bold
   488→Body: 16px, regular
   489→BodySmall: 14px, regular
   490→Caption: 12px, regular
   491→```
   492→
   493→**Button sizes:**
   494→```
   495→Large: 48px height, 16px horizontal padding
   496→Medium: 40px height, 12px horizontal padding
   497→Small: 32px height, 8px horizontal padding
   498→```
   499→
   500→---
   501→
   502→## Accessibility Notes
   503→
   504→- All text must have sufficient contrast (WCAG AA minimum)
   505→- Touch targets minimum 44x44 pixels
   506→- Radio buttons should be large and clearly labeled
   507→- Error messages should be clear and specific
   508→- Screen reader support (alt text for images, semantic HTML)
   509→- Color not the only indicator (use icons, text, patterns)
   510→
   511→---
   512→
   513→## Mobile-First Considerations
   514→
   515→- Test on small screens (iPhone SE = 375px width)
   516→- Avoid horizontal scrolling
   517→- Buttons and inputs should be thumb-friendly
   518→- Font sizes should be ≥ 16px to prevent zoom on iOS
   519→- Avoid fixed positioning (use ScrollView)
   520→- Handle keyboard appearance (keyboard avoidance for inputs)
   521→
   522→---
   523→
   524→## v0.1 Constraints
   525→
   526→- No animations (keep it simple)
   527→- No custom fonts (use system fonts)
   528→- No images or complex graphics
   529→- Minimal colors (stick to design tokens)
   530→- No complex layouts (stack vertically)
   531→- No responsive breakpoints (mobile-first, simple scaling)
   532→
   533→---
   534→
   535→## Testing Checklist
   536→
   537→- [ ] All screens render without errors
   538→- [ ] Text is readable on small screens
   539→- [ ] Buttons are touchable (44px minimum)
   540→- [ ] Navigation works (Previous/Next, buttons)
   541→- [ ] Auto-save works during assessment
   542→- [ ] Results display correctly
   543→- [ ] Error messages appear on failed login/register
   544→- [ ] Logout works and clears session
   545→

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

file.content:
# UI/UX Screens Specification (v0.1)

**Platform:** React Native (Expo)  
**Styling:** React Native built-in StyleSheet  
**Target:** Personal use, mobile-first (phone testing)  
**Philosophy:** Functional first, beautiful later. Clear, readable, touch-friendly.

---

## Screen Hierarchy & Navigation Flow

```
Splash/Loading
  ↓
┌──────────────────────────────┐
│    NOT AUTHENTICATED         │
├──────────────────────────────┤
│  LoginScreen                 │
│  RegisterScreen              │
│  ↓ (on success)              │
└──────────────────────────────┘
  ↓
┌──────────────────────────────┐
│    AUTHENTICATED             │
├──────────────────────────────┤
│  HomeScreen                  │
│  ├─→ AssessmentScreen        │
│  │   ├─→ ResultsScreen       │
│  │   └─→ (back to Home)      │
│  └─→ Logout (back to Login)  │
└──────────────────────────────┘
```

---

## 1. SplashScreen (Optional, ~2 seconds)

**Purpose:** Show loading state while app initializes

**Elements:**
- App logo or title: "Attachment Profile"
- Loading spinner
- Optional tagline: "Understanding your relationship patterns"

**Behavior:**
- Check if user is authenticated (token in AsyncStorage)
- If yes: navigate to HomeScreen
- If no: navigate to LoginScreen
- Auto-hide after 2 seconds or when auth check completes

---

## 2. LoginScreen

**Purpose:** Authenticate existing user

**Elements:**
1. **Header section:**
   - Title: "Welcome back"
   - Subtitle: "Sign in to your account"

2. **Email input field:**
   - Placeholder: "you@example.com"
   - Keyboard: email-address
   - Validation: basic email format check

3. **Password input field:**
   - Placeholder: "Password"
   - secureTextEntry: true
   - Show/hide password icon (optional)

4. **"Login" button:**
   - Primary color (indigo)
   - Full width, padded
   - Shows spinner while loading

5. **Error message display:**
   - Red text, visible only if login fails
   - Possible messages:
     - "Invalid email or password"
     - "Please enter valid email"
     - "Password required"
     - "Network error - please try again"

6. **Link to RegisterScreen:**
   - "Don't have an account? Register"
   - Secondary text color, underlined

**Layout Example:**
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────┐
│                                     │
│      Welcome back                   │
│      Sign in to your account        │
│                                     │
│   ┌─────────────────────────────┐   │
│   │ you@example.com             │   │
│   └─────────────────────────────┘   │
│                                     │
│   ┌─────────────────────────────┐   │
│   │ ••••••••••••••              │   │
│   └─────────────────────────────┘   │
│                                     │
│   ┌─────────────────────────────┐   │
│   │       Login      [🔄]       │   │
│   └─────────────────────────────┘   │
│                                     │
│   Don't have an account? Register   │
│                                     │
└─────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Behavior:**
- Validate email format before submit
- Show error if email/password incorrect
- Navigate to HomeScreen on success
- Store JWT token in AsyncStorage
- Optional: Remember email in local storage

**Styling:**
- Background: white
- Input borders: light gray (1px)
- Button: primary color (indigo)
- Text: dark gray
- Error text: red (#EF4444)
- Links: primary color, underlined

---

## 3. RegisterScreen

**Purpose:** Create new user account

**Elements:**
1. **Header section:**
   - Title: "Create account"
   - Subtitle: "Get started with your attachment journey"

2. **Email input field**

3. **Password input field**

4. **Confirm password input field:**
   - Placeholder: "Confirm password"
   - Must match password field

5. **"Create account" button**

6. **Validation feedback:**
   - Below each field (optional, live)
   - Show if password is weak
   - Show if passwords don't match

7. **Link to LoginScreen:**
   - "Already have an account? Login"

**Layout Example:**
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────┐
│                                     │
│      Create account                 │
│      Get started on your journey    │
│                                     │
│   ┌─────────────────────────────┐   │
│   │ you@example.com             │   │
│   └─────────────────────────────┘   │
│                                     │
│   ┌─────────────────────────────┐   │
│   │ ••••••••••••••              │   │
│   └─────────────────────────────┘   │
│   ⚠ At least 8 characters           │
│                                     │
│   ┌─────────────────────────────┐   │
│   │ ••••••••••••••              │   │
│   └─────────────────────────────┘   │
│                                     │
│   ┌─────────────────────────────┐   │
│   │   Create account [🔄]      │   │
│   └─────────────────────────────┘   │
│                                     │
│   Already have an account? Login    │
│                                     │
└─────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Validation Rules:**
- Email: valid format required
- Password: ≥ 8 characters
- Confirm: must match password exactly
- Real-time feedback: green checkmark when valid

**Error Messages:**
- ❌ "Email already in use"
- ❌ "Password must be at least 8 characters"
- ❌ "Passwords do not match"
- ❌ "Invalid email format"
- ✅ "Account created successfully!"

**Behavior:**
- Disable "Create account" button until all fields valid
- Auto-login after registration (optional: show "Logging you in...")
- Navigate to HomeScreen on success

---

## 4. HomeScreen

**Purpose:** Main entry point; gateway to assessment and results

**Elements:**
1. **Header section:**
   - Greeting: "Hi, [user's email]"
   - Title: "Your Attachment Profile"

2. **Primary action button:**
   - "Start Assessment" (or "Resume Assessment" if in progress)
   - Large, primary color, full width
   - Icon: play button or pencil icon

3. **Secondary action button:**
   - "View Past Results" (disabled if no completed assessments)
   - Secondary color, full width
   - Icon: chart/graph icon

4. **Status section (if assessment in progress):**
   - "You have an in-progress assessment"
   - Shows last question number: "Question 18 of 36"
   - "Continue" button or "Start over" link

5. **Logout button:**
   - Link text in footer
   - Small, secondary color

**Layout Example:**
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Hi, user@example.com               │
│                                     │
│  Your Attachment Profile            │
│                                     │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │ ▶ Start Assessment          │   │
│  └─────────────────────────────┘   │
│                                     │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │ 📊 View Past Results        │   │
│  │    (disabled)               │   │
│  └─────────────────────────────┘   │
│                                     │
│  Or resume: Question 18 of 36       │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │       Continue              │   │
│  └─────────────────────────────┘   │
│                                     │
│           Logout                    │
│                                     │
└─────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Behavior:**
- Check for in-progress assessment on load
- If exists: show "Resume" option with last question number
- "Start Assessment" → navigate to AssessmentScreen
- "View Past Results" → (disabled for v0.1, enabled in v0.2)
- "Logout" → clear token, return to LoginScreen
- Optional: show last completion date if assessment completed

---

## 5. AssessmentScreen

**Purpose:** Answer ECR-R questions one by one

**Elements:**
1. **Progress section:**
   - Progress bar (visual fill: 5/36 complete)
   - Question counter: "Question 5 of 36"
   - Percentage: "14% complete"

2. **Question section:**
   - Question text (large, centered, readable)
   - Font size: 16-18px
   - Padding: adequate margins

3. **Response section (7-point Likert scale):**
   - 7 radio buttons or touch-friendly buttons
   - Labels: "Strongly Disagree" ← → "Strongly Agree"
   - Visual indication of selected answer (highlighted)
   - Current selection shown clearly

4. **Navigation section:**
   - "Previous" button (disabled on Q1)
   - "Next" button (disabled until answer selected)
   - Optional: "Skip to Results" (NO - don't implement, force completion)

5. **Optional features:**
   - Save indicator: "Auto-saved" (shown briefly after selection)
   - Question-specific help text (not needed for v0.1)

**Layout Example:**
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────┐
│                                     │
│   Question 5 of 36                  │
│   ████░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░ 14%   │
│                                     │
│  ─────────────────────────────────  │
│                                     │
│  "I worry a fair amount about       │
│   losing my partner."               │
│                                     │
│  ─────────────────────────────────  │
│                                     │
│  ◯ 1  Strongly Disagree             │
│  ◯ 2  Disagree                      │
│  ◯ 3  Somewhat Disagree             │
│  ◉ 4  Neutral                       │
│  ◯ 5  Somewhat Agree                │
│  ◯ 6  Agree                         │
│  ◯ 7  Strongly Agree                │
│                                     │
│  ┌──────────────┬──────────────┐   │
│  │  Previous    │    Next      │   │
│  └──────────────┴──────────────┘   │
│                                     │
│  Auto-saved                         │
│                                     │
└─────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Behavior:**
- Display one question at a time
- Auto-save selection to AsyncStorage after each answer
- Previous button goes back (preserves answer)
- Next button goes forward (only enabled if answered)
- Progress bar updates with each question
- On Q36: "Next" button changes to "Submit" or "See Results"
- Handle screen rotations gracefully (optional)

**Styling:**
- Background: white or very light gray
- Radio buttons: clear, large touch targets (44px minimum)
- Progress bar: primary color
- Selected state: primary color highlight
- Typography: readable, high contrast

---

## 6. ResultsScreen

**Purpose:** Display attachment scores and interpretation

**Elements:**
1. **Header section:**
   - Title: "Your Results"
   - Subtitle: "Understanding your attachment style"
   - Optional: Date completed

2. **Scores section:**
   - **Anxiety Score:**
     - Large number: "4.2"
     - Label: "Anxiety" with brief definition
     - Visual bar (1-7 scale, color-coded)
   
   - **Avoidance Score:**
     - Large number: "3.8"
     - Label: "Avoidance" with brief definition
     - Visual bar (1-7 scale, color-coded)

3. **Attachment Style section:**
   - Large, prominent label: "Secure" (or other style)
   - Color-coded background (optional)
   - Icon (optional): ❤️ for secure, etc.

4. **Interpretation section:**
   - 3-5 sentences of warm, non-clinical interpretation
   - Tailored to attachment style
   - Accessible language (no jargon)

5. **Growth tip section:**
   - One actionable suggestion
   - Specific to attachment style
   - Positive, growth-oriented

6. **Action buttons:**
   - "Back to Home" button
   - "Retake Assessment" button (secondary)
   - "Share Results" (optional, v0.2)

**Layout Example:**
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────┐
│         Your Results                │
│     Understanding your style        │
│     Completed: Feb 6, 2025         │
│                                     │
│  ─────────────────────────────────  │
│                                     │
│        Anxiety: 4.2                 │
│  ████████░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░  │
│                                     │
│        Avoidance: 3.8               │
│  ███████░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░  │
│                                     │
│  ─────────────────────────────────  │
│                                     │
│            ❤️ SECURE                │
│                                     │
│  You generally feel comfortable     │
│  with emotional intimacy and can    │
│  balance your needs with your       │
│  partner's. You tend toward trust   │
│  and healthy interdependence.       │
│                                     │
│  💡 Growth Tip:                     │
│  Practice occasional vulnerability │
│  sharing to deepen connection.      │
│                                     │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │    Back to Home             │   │
│  └─────────────────────────────┘   │
│                                     │
│       Retake Assessment             │
│                                     │
└─────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Interpretation Text (Example for Secure):**
```
"Your attachment pattern suggests you're secure in relationships. 
You're generally comfortable with both closeness and autonomy, 
and you tend to communicate your needs clearly. You likely feel 
secure enough to be vulnerable when appropriate, and you balance 
your own needs with your partner's. This is a strong foundation 
for healthy, interdependent relationships."
```

**Growth Tips (by style):**
- **Secure:** "Practice occasional vulnerability to deepen intimacy."
- **Anxious-Preoccupied:** "Notice when seeking reassurance becomes a habit; practice self-soothing first."
- **Dismissive-Avoidant:** "Create small moments of emotional sharing; vulnerability strengthens bonds."
- **Fearful-Avoidant:** "Work on self-compassion; your conflicted feelings are valid and can be understood."

**Styling:**
- Background: white or soft gradient
- Scores: large, bold typography
- Attachment style: prominent, color-coded (optional)
- Interpretation: warm tone, readable contrast
- Buttons: clear call-to-action

**Behavior:**
- Calculate scores automatically (backend + frontend verification)
- Display results immediately after submission
- Allow returning to HomeScreen
- Optional: download or share results (v0.2)

---

## Design Tokens (Consistent Across All Screens)

**Colors:**
```
Primary: #4F46E5 (Indigo)
Success: #10B981 (Green)
Warning: #F59E0B (Amber)
Error: #EF4444 (Red)
Background: #FFFFFF (White)
Surface: #F3F4F6 (Light Gray)
Text: #111827 (Dark)
TextSecondary: #6B7280 (Gray)
Border: #D1D5DB (Light Gray)
```

**Spacing:**
```
xs: 4px
sm: 8px
md: 16px
lg: 24px
xl: 32px
```

**Typography:**
```
HeadingXL: 32px, bold
HeadingL: 24px, bold
HeadingM: 20px, bold
Body: 16px, regular
BodySmall: 14px, regular
Caption: 12px, regular
```

**Button sizes:**
```
Large: 48px height, 16px horizontal padding
Medium: 40px height, 12px horizontal padding
Small: 32px height, 8px horizontal padding
```

---

## Accessibility Notes

- All text must have sufficient contrast (WCAG AA minimum)
- Touch targets minimum 44x44 pixels
- Radio buttons should be large and clearly labeled
- Error messages should be clear and specific
- Screen reader support (alt text for images, semantic HTML)
- Color not the only indicator (use icons, text, patterns)

---

## Mobile-First Considerations

- Test on small screens (iPhone SE = 375px width)
- Avoid horizontal scrolling
- Buttons and inputs should be thumb-friendly
- Font sizes should be ≥ 16px to prevent zoom on iOS
- Avoid fixed positioning (use ScrollView)
- Handle keyboard appearance (keyboard avoidance for inputs)

---

## v0.1 Constraints

- No animations (keep it simple)
- No custom fonts (use system fonts)
- No images or complex graphics
- Minimal colors (stick to design tokens)
- No complex layouts (stack vertically)
- No responsive breakpoints (mobile-first, simple scaling)

---

## Testing Checklist

- [ ] All screens render without errors
- [ ] Text is readable on small screens
- [ ] Buttons are touchable (44px minimum)
- [ ] Navigation works (Previous/Next, buttons)
- [ ] Auto-save works during assessment
- [ ] Results display correctly
- [ ] Error messages appear on failed login/register
- [ ] Logout works and clears session

content.text:
I've read all 5 context files. Here's what I understand:

**The app:** A React Native (Expo) couples attachment assessment tool using the ECR-R (36 questions), with auth, scoring, and results display.

**Current state of this project:** You already have a working Expo project (SDK 54) with expo-router, Supabase client installed, and your Supabase credentials in `.env.local`. The existing scaffold uses TypeScript and expo-router (file-based routing with tabs), which is slightly different from what the specs describe (plain JS, react-navigation stack). 

Now — per the prompt, I need to ask clarifying questions before I start building. Based on the specs and your README answers, I already know most of your preferences, but let me confirm a few things:

**Phase 1 Questions:**

1. **Backend approach:** Your specs mention both "Node.js/Express backend" and "Supabase." I see you already have Supabase credentials set up. The README says use Supabase directly (no separate Node.js server). **Should I skip the Express backend entirely and just use Supabase directly from the app** (auth + database)? This is the simplest path.

2. **Expo Router vs React Navigation:** This project was scaffolded with `expo-router` (file-based routing, TypeScript). Your specs describe `react-navigation` with plain JavaScript. **Should I keep expo-router + TypeScript** (it's already set up and is the modern Expo standard), or do you want me to convert to the simpler stack from the specs?

3. **Existing tab layout:** The current scaffold has a tab-based layout. Your app needs a stack-based flow (Login → Home → Assessment → Results). **I'll replace the tabs with a stack navigator** — just confirming that's fine.

4. **Styling:** Confirmed — React Native built-in StyleSheet, functional first, pretty later.

What are your answers on 1-3? Then I'll start building.

content.text:
Got it. Clear answers on everything. Let me summarize the approach:

- **Supabase directly** (no Express backend) — auth + database
- **Keep expo-router + TypeScript** — modern, already set up
- **Stack-based flow** — replace tabs
- **Build with the long game in mind** — clean architecture that scales to v0.2+ (more assessments, reports, partner mode)

Let me start building. I'll do **Phase 2: Project Structure** first — set up the foundation properly so future assessments slot in cleanly.

**Important:** I noticed you pasted your Supabase secret key above. That key should stay private (server-side only). For the client app, we only need the publishable/anon key, which is already in your `.env.local`. The secret key is in `.env.local.server` where it belongs.

Let me scaffold the project structure now.

4. Create the type definitions (future-proof for multiple assessments)
5. Create the questions data file
6. Create the scoring utility
7. Restructure the app routes (remove tabs, set up auth flow)

Let me do it all now.

input.content:
import { Question } from '@/types';

/**
 * ECR-R Assessment — 36 questions
 * Source: Fraley, Shaver, & Brennan (2000)
 *
 * Items 1-18: Anxiety subscale
 * Items 19-36: Avoidance subscale
 * Reverse-scored items: 20, 21, 30, 33, 34, 35 (1-indexed)
 */
export const ECR_R_QUESTIONS: Question[] = [
  // ─── Anxiety Subscale (Items 1-18) ──────────────────────
  { id: 1, text: "I'm afraid that I will lose my partner's love.", subscale: 'anxiety', reverseScored: false },
  { id: 2, text: "I often worry that my partner will not want to stay with me.", subscale: 'anxiety', reverseScored: false },
  { id: 3, text: "I worry that my partner doesn't care about me as much as I care about them.", subscale: 'anxiety', reverseScored: false },
  { id: 4, text: "I worry a fair amount about losing my partner.", subscale: 'anxiety', reverseScored: false },
  { id: 5, text: "My desire to be very close sometimes scares people away.", subscale: 'anxiety', reverseScored: false },
  { id: 6, text: "I'm afraid that once my partner gets to know me, he or she won't like who I really am.", subscale: 'anxiety', reverseScored: false },
  { id: 7, text: "I worry that my partner won't care about me as much as I care about him or her.", subscale: 'anxiety', reverseScored: false },
  { id: 8, text: "I often wish that my partner's feelings were as deep as mine.", subscale: 'anxiety', reverseScored: false },
  { id: 9, text: "I worry that I won't measure up to other people my partner knows.", subscale: 'anxiety', reverseScored: false },
  { id: 10, text: "My partner only seems to want to be close to me when I initiate it.", subscale: 'anxiety', reverseScored: false },
  { id: 11, text: "I find that my partner(s) don't want to get as close as I would like.", subscale: 'anxiety', reverseScored: false },
  { id: 12, text: "Sometimes I feel that I force my partner to show more feeling, more commitment.", subscale: 'anxiety', reverseScored: false },
  { id: 13, text: "I tell my partner that I love him or her too often or more than he or she says it to me.", subscale: 'anxiety', reverseScored: false },
  { id: 14, text: "I find myself needing reassurance from my partner that we're okay.", subscale: 'anxiety', reverseScored: false },
  { id: 15, text: "I need a lot of reassurance that I am loved and valued in my relationship.", subscale: 'anxiety', reverseScored: false },
  { id: 16, text: "I worry about being abandoned.", subscale: 'anxiety', reverseScored: false },
  { id: 17, text: "I get upset when my partner is unavailable or seems uninterested in me.", subscale: 'anxiety', reverseScored: false },
  { id: 18, text: "My jealousy or anger sometimes makes it difficult for my partner to stay close to me.", subscale: 'anxiety', reverseScored: false },

  // ─── Avoidance Subscale (Items 19-36) ───────────────────
  { id: 19, text: "I prefer not to show a partner how I feel deep down.", subscale: 'avoidance', reverseScored: false },
  { id: 20, text: "I feel comfortable opening up to my partner and talking about my fears.", subscale: 'avoidance', reverseScored: true },
  { id: 21, text: "It helps to turn to my romantic partner in times of need.", subscale: 'avoidance', reverseScored: true },
  { id: 22, text: "I prefer not to be too close to romantic partners.", subscale: 'avoidance', reverseScored: false },
  { id: 23, text: "I get uncomfortable when a romantic partner wants to be very close.", subscale: 'avoidance', reverseScored: false },
  { id: 24, text: "I prefer not to depend on a romantic partner.", subscale: 'avoidance', reverseScored: false },
  { id: 25, text: "I don't feel comfortable depending on romantic partners.", subscale: 'avoidance', reverseScored: false },
  { id: 26, text: "I'm uncomfortable when romantic partners want to be too emotionally close.", subscale: 'avoidance', reverseScored: false },
  { id: 27, text: "I want to get close to my partner, but I keep pulling back.", subscale: 'avoidance', reverseScored: false },
  { id: 28, text: "I am nervous when partners get too close, and I want them to back off.", subscale: 'avoidance', reverseScored: false },
  { id: 29, text: "I feel that it does not make a difference whether I'm with a partner or alone.", subscale: 'avoidance', reverseScored: false },
  { id: 30, text: "My partner really understands me and my needs.", subscale: 'avoidance', reverseScored: true },
  { id: 31, text: "I think it is important to maintain some emotional distance in relationships.", subscale: 'avoidance', reverseScored: false },
  { id: 32, text: "I find it difficult to allow myself to depend completely on romantic partners.", subscale: 'avoidance', reverseScored: false },
  { id: 33, text: "I am very comfortable being close to romantic partners.", subscale: 'avoidance', reverseScored: true },
  { id: 34, text: "I don't mind asking intimate partners for comfort or help.", subscale: 'avoidance', reverseScored: true },
  { id: 35, text: "It's easy for me to be affectionate with my partner.", subscale: 'avoidance', reverseScored: true },
  { id: 36, text: "My partner makes me doubt myself.", subscale: 'avoidance', reverseScored: false },
];

export const LIKERT_LABELS = [
  { value: 1, label: 'Strongly Disagree' },
  { value: 2, label: 'Disagree' },
  { value: 3, label: 'Somewhat Disagree' },
  { value: 4, label: 'Neutral' },
  { value: 5, label: 'Somewhat Agree' },
  { value: 6, label: 'Agree' },
  { value: 7, label: 'Strongly Agree' },
];

content.text:
TypeScript passes clean — zero errors.

Now, there's one important step: **you need to create the `ecr_r_assessments` table in Supabase**. Here's what you need to do:

---

**Go to your Supabase dashboard** → SQL Editor → New Query, and run this:

```sql
-- Create the assessments table
CREATE TABLE ecr_r_assessments (
  id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
  user_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES auth.users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  responses JSONB NOT NULL,
  anxiety_score FLOAT,
  avoidance_score FLOAT,
  attachment_style VARCHAR(50),
  completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
);

-- Enable Row Level Security
ALTER TABLE ecr_r_assessments ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

-- Users can only see their own assessments
CREATE POLICY "Users can view own assessments"
  ON ecr_r_assessments FOR SELECT
  USING (auth.uid() = user_id);

-- Users can insert their own assessments
CREATE POLICY "Users can insert own assessments"
  ON ecr_r_assessments FOR INSERT
  WITH CHECK (auth.uid() = user_id);
```

---

Once you've run that SQL, tell me and I'll start the app so we can test. 

Also — **one quick Supabase auth setting to check:** In your Supabase dashboard → Authentication → Settings → make sure "Enable email confirmations" is set to how you want it. If you want to skip email verification for testing, you can disable it there. Otherwise, new users will need to click a confirmation email link before they can log in.

Should I proceed with testing while you set up the table, or do you want to do that first?
Let me start the app so we can test it on your phone and browser.

content.text:
Expo is running on **http://localhost:8081**. 

The app is live. Here's what you can do now:

1. **On your phone:** Open Expo Go and scan the QR code from your terminal
2. **In browser:** Go to `http://localhost:8081` (though web has a known AsyncStorage/SSR issue — phone is the primary target)

**What you should see:**
- A splash screen saying "Attachment Profile" for a moment
- Then it redirects to the **Login screen** ("Welcome back" / "Sign in to your account")
- You can tap "Register" to create an account
- After registering + verifying email (or if you disabled email confirmation in Supabase), you'll land on the **Home screen**
- "Start Assessment" takes you through all 36 questions
- Completing them shows your **Results** with scores, style, interpretation, and growth tip

**Quick question about Supabase email verification:** Did you disable "Confirm email" in Supabase Auth settings? If it's still on, you'll need to check your email after registering to click the verification link before you can log in. If you want to skip that for testing, go to Supabase → Authentication → Providers → Email → turn off "Confirm email."

Go ahead and test it — try registering and let me know what happens! I'll wait for your feedback before moving on.

content.text:
Found the problem. The `AsyncStorage` library doesn't work in the web SSR (server-side rendering) context that expo-router uses. The Supabase client initializes at import time and tries to access `window` before the browser environment is ready.

I need to fix the Supabase client to handle both web and native properly. Let me fix this.

lines: +// AsyncStorage doesn't work in SSR/Node — use a safe wrapper
+  // On web SSR (Node), there's no window — use in-memory fallback
+  // On native, use AsyncStorage (lazy import to avoid SSR crash)

3. Also go to **Authentication** → **Users** — you'll probably see your test accounts listed there as "unconfirmed"

**OR** the faster fix — let me update the app so that after registration, it shows a clear message and redirects to login. But first, let's just get the Supabase setting right.

1. Screenshot or confirm the "Confirm email" toggle is truly OFF
2. Check **Authentication → Users** — do you see any users listed there?

If you see unconfirmed users, you can either delete them and re-register (with confirm email OFF), or manually confirm them from the dashboard.

content.text:
Now three key fixes are in:
1. **AuthContext** — explicitly sets session after signup/signin (not relying on listener)
2. **Register** — explicitly navigates to home on success
3. **Login** — explicitly navigates to home on success

Now — first **delete any existing test users** from Supabase dashboard → Authentication → Users. Then reload the app and try registering with a **fresh email**. It should take you straight to the Home screen.

Auth is fully working — register, login, and logout all confirmed. Let's move on.

**Now test the assessment flow.** From the Home screen, tap **"Start Assessment"**. You should see:
- Question 1 of 36 with a progress bar
- The question text in italics
- 7 radio buttons (Strongly Disagree → Strongly Agree)
- Previous/Next buttons

Answer a few questions and let me know how it feels. You don't need to do all 36 right now — just test that:
1. Questions load and you can select answers
2. Next/Previous buttons work
3. Progress bar updates

Then tell me how it's going and I'll wait for you.

content.text:
**v0.1 is complete!** Here's what's working:

| Feature | Status |
|---------|--------|
| Register with email/password | ✅ |
| Login/Logout | ✅ |
| All 36 ECR-R questions | ✅ |
| 7-point Likert scale | ✅ |
| Progress bar + question counter | ✅ |
| Previous/Next navigation | ✅ |
| Auto-save progress (AsyncStorage) | ✅ |
| ECR-R scoring (anxiety + avoidance) | ✅ |
| Attachment style determination | ✅ |
| Warm interpretation + growth tip | ✅ |
| Data saved to Supabase | ✅ |
| Works on phone + browser | ✅ |

**What's next?** Based on your roadmap:

- **v0.2:** Chat with Claude (send portrait context to Claude API for personalized conversations about attachment patterns)
- **v0.3:** Add more assessments (Big Five, EI, DSI-R, DUTCH)
- **v0.4:** Partner mode (invite your girlfriend, dyadic portrait)

Would you like to keep going with any improvements to v0.1 first (UI polish, bug fixes, retake flow), or are you ready to start planning v0.2?
